1.anchor: I am writing to all who have been called by God the Father, who loves you and keeps you safe in the care of Jesus Christ.
1.note: Or keeps you for Jesus Christ.

5.anchor: So I want to remind you, though you already know these things, that Jesus
5.note: Other manuscripts read [the] Lord, God, God Christ.

8.anchor: In the same way, these people—who claim authority from their dreams—live immoral lives, defy authority, and scoff at supernatural beings.

9.anchor: But even Michael, one of the mightiest of the angels,

12.anchor: When these people eat with you in your fellowship meals commemorating the Lord’s love, they are like dangerous reefs that can shipwreck you.
12.note: Or they are contaminants among you; they are stains.

14.anchor: to execute judgment on the people of the world. He will convict every person of all the ungodly things they have done and for all the insults that ungodly sinners have spoken against him.”
14.note: The quotation comes from intertestamental literature: 1 Enoch 1:9.

20.anchor: But you, dear friends, must build each other up in your most holy faith, pray in the power of the Holy Spirit,
20.note: Greek pray in the Holy Spirit.

22.anchor: And you must show mercy to
Rescue others by snatching them from the flames of judgment. Show mercy to still others,
22.note: Some manuscripts have only two categories of people: (1) those whose faith is wavering and therefore need to be snatched from the flames of judgment, and (2) those who need to be shown mercy.

23.anchor: but do so with great caution, hating the sins that contaminate their lives.
23.note: Greek with fear, hating even the clothing stained by the flesh.
